config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya karena alasan kepentingan সমাজ?

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States we have learned that “the common good” can be used to justify abuse if individual rights are not protected first. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every case: laws on public safety, infectious disease, and fraud show that a society cannot function if everyone ignores shared rules. For me, the real principle is that society can limit individual freedom only when the limit is narrow, necessary, and accountable. If the balance goes the other way, people’s rights become too easy to sacrifice in the name of convenience or majority rule.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal hak warga justru harus jadi pagar agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi alasan untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau minoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, I am very wary of any argument that starts with "for the greater good," because history shows how easily that becomes a license to trample minorities and justify overreach by government or majorities. That said, I do think there are limited cases where society can impose narrow, accountable restrictions for public safety or public health, but those limits have to be carefully justified and never treated as a blank check. If we reverse the default and put society first, individual rights stop being rights and become privileges that can be withdrawn whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Di Indonesia, saya melihat istilah “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk melegitimasi pembatasan yang melampaui batas, dan akibatnya justru warga kecil atau minoritas yang paling mudah dirugikan. Memang ada pengecualian untuk kesehatan publik, keamanan, atau keadaan darurat, tetapi itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diawasi, bukan dijadikan alasan serba boleh. Kalau hak individu dijadikan nomor dua, maka yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat mudah berubah menjadi kepentingan pihak yang paling berkuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American point of view, I am especially skeptical of that claim because “society first” has too often been used to excuse overreach, silence dissent, or sideline minorities, even when the majority feels justified. The better standard is that society can limit individual rights only in narrow, necessary, and accountable ways, such as public health or safety, and even then the burden of proof should be high. If we make society the default priority, rights stop being real protections and become conditional privileges.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
